mainQuestTheme: Recognizing recurring relational, instinctive, and collective patterns before old fear turns recognition into control.

hero.atmosphericSubtitle: The pattern appears before the proof does.

questBrief.mission: Notice the returning pattern without turning one signal into certainty.
questBrief.fieldPrompt: Find the familiar trace inside the current situation.
questBrief.livePrompt: What feels familiar before you can prove it?

fieldBriefing.collapsedPreview: Do not overread the first signal.
fieldBriefing.whatThisQuestIsAbout: This quest begins with instinctive recognition. Something in the field feels familiar, but the first signal is only a trace, not the whole pattern.
fieldBriefing.whyThisMattersNow: Early recognition is powerful when it stays clean. It becomes fear when one familiar detail is treated as a final verdict.
fieldBriefing.primaryRecognitionTrigger: You feel one familiar thing and decide you already know the whole story.
fieldBriefing.livePrompt: Where are you turning recognition into conclusion too fast?

frictionSignals: reacting to a past pattern too early
assuming repetition before enough evidence appears
assetsAndFriction.livePrompt: Watch where the past starts speaking louder than the present.

groundingProtocol: relax the back of the neck
groundingEffect.somaticShift: The body can sense familiarity without bracing against it.
groundingEffect.bodyCheckPrompt: Is your belly sensing, or already preparing for defense?

unlockCondition.questKey: Notice the familiar signal → wait for a second marker → name the pattern only after confirmation
unlockCondition.embodiedSignal: The body stays alert without tightening into fear.
unlockCondition.ritualPrompt: Write the first signal down without deciding what it means yet.
unlockCondition.alignmentReminder: A familiar trace is not the full pattern.
unlockCondition.livePrompt: Let recognition gather one more marker.

hero.atmosphericSubtitle: The body recognizes the pattern before the mind explains it.

questBrief.mission: Trust the instinctive read without adding fear or performance.
questBrief.fieldPrompt: Notice what your body recognizes before your mind builds a case.
questBrief.livePrompt: What do you know before you explain it?

fieldBriefing.whatThisQuestIsAbout: This line recognizes patterns naturally. The body catches continuity, motive, or repetition before the mind can explain how it knows.
fieldBriefing.whyThisMattersNow: The gift is clean recognition. The risk is contaminating the read with fear, suspicion, or the need to prove it.
fieldBriefing.primaryRecognitionTrigger: You know the pattern, then start making it sound worse so people believe you.
fieldBriefing.livePrompt: Where does your read get distorted when you try to prove it?

assetsAndFriction.questTrap: You exaggerate the signal to make the read defensible.
frictionSignals: adding suspicion to a clean read
overexplaining what the body already knows
turning recognition into warning too fast

groundingEffect.somaticShift: The body keeps the read intact without escalating it.
groundingEffect.bodyCheckPrompt: Does your throat tighten when you try to convince someone?

unlockCondition.questKey: Feel the pattern → keep the read clean → speak only what is actually present
unlockCondition.embodiedSignal: The body feels alert but not alarmed.
unlockCondition.ritualPrompt: Say the clean observation without adding a prediction.
unlockCondition.alignmentReminder: Instinct loses accuracy when fear decorates it.
unlockCondition.livePrompt: Name the pattern without amplifying it.

hero.atmosphericSubtitle: The pattern reveals itself through repeated contact.

questBrief.mission: Use each encounter to refine the read instead of repeating the same reaction.
questBrief.fieldPrompt: Track what repeats across different interactions.

fieldBriefing.whatThisQuestIsAbout: This line learns patterns through encounter, mistake, friction, and repetition. The read improves when each contact updates the instinct instead of confirming an old fear.
fieldBriefing.whyThisMattersNow: Repeated patterns are data. Repeated reactions are loops.
fieldBriefing.primaryRecognitionTrigger: You keep meeting the same pattern and acting surprised every time.
fieldBriefing.livePrompt: Where is the lesson repeating because the response has not changed?

assetsAndFriction.questTrap: You repeat the reaction instead of updating the read.
assetsAndFriction.livePrompt: Watch where the pattern is training you and you are not adapting.

groundingEffect.somaticShift: The body can register the encounter without snapping into the old response.
groundingEffect.bodyCheckPrompt: Are you responding to this moment, or to every time it happened before?

unlockCondition.questKey: Identify the repeat → name the old reaction → choose one different response
unlockCondition.embodiedSignal: The body feels the familiar pull but does not obey it automatically.
unlockCondition.ritualPrompt: Before responding, write what pattern this reminds you of.
unlockCondition.alignmentReminder: Recognition only matters if the response evolves.
unlockCondition.livePrompt: Let the repeated pattern change your next move.

hero.atmosphericSubtitle: Patterns become clearer when the field confirms them.

questBrief.mission: Name the pattern in a way others can recognize without forcing agreement.
questBrief.fieldPrompt: Find the shared pattern moving through the group.

fieldBriefing.whatThisQuestIsAbout: This line recognizes patterns through groups, relationships, teams, families, or communities. The pattern is not only personal; it lives in the field between people.
fieldBriefing.whyThisMattersNow: Shared recognition can create real change. Forced recognition creates resistance.
fieldBriefing.primaryRecognitionTrigger: You see the group pattern, then carry it alone because naming it feels risky.
fieldBriefing.livePrompt: Where is everyone affected, but no one has named the pattern?

assetsAndFriction.questTrap: You hold the field’s memory without giving it a voice.
frictionSignals: waiting for others to recognize what you already see
assetsAndFriction.livePrompt: Watch where silence keeps the old pattern running.

groundingEffect.somaticShift: The body stays connected while naming what the field has been avoiding.
groundingEffect.bodyCheckPrompt: Can your chest stay open while you name the pattern?

unlockCondition.questKey: Identify the shared pattern → speak one clear observation → let the field respond
unlockCondition.embodiedSignal: The body stays steady without needing everyone to agree immediately.
unlockCondition.ritualPrompt: Say what you notice as an observation, not an accusation.
unlockCondition.alignmentReminder: A pattern named cleanly does not need to be forced.
unlockCondition.livePrompt: Put the pattern into the room.

hero.atmosphericSubtitle: People trust your read before checking whether it is clean.

questBrief.mission: Use pattern recognition responsibly when others treat your read as authority.
questBrief.fieldPrompt: Separate what you know from what others want you to know.
questBrief.livePrompt: Who is giving your read too much weight?

fieldBriefing.whatThisQuestIsAbout: This line can become trusted for recognizing patterns, motives, or recurring dynamics. Others may project authority onto the read before the evidence is complete.
fieldBriefing.whyThisMattersNow: A strong read can shape decisions. If fear, pride, or projection enters, the pattern becomes distorted through influence.
fieldBriefing.primaryRecognitionTrigger: You let your read sound final because they want certainty.
fieldBriefing.livePrompt: Where are you turning a strong read into a verdict?

assetsAndFriction.questTrap: You become the pattern judge instead of the pattern reader.
frictionSignals: letting others treat instinct as verdict
assetsAndFriction.livePrompt: Watch where being trusted pressures the read to harden.

groundingEffect.somaticShift: The body stops rising into authority and returns to measured recognition.
groundingEffect.bodyCheckPrompt: Are you reading the pattern, or performing certainty?

unlockCondition.questKey: Name the pattern → mark the evidence limit → refuse to turn instinct into verdict
unlockCondition.embodiedSignal: The body feels grounded when the read stays proportionate.
unlockCondition.ritualPrompt: Say what is known, then say what is not yet proven.
unlockCondition.alignmentReminder: A powerful read still needs limits.
unlockCondition.livePrompt: Keep the read clean enough to be trusted.

hero.atmosphericSubtitle: The pattern is old, but the response does not have to be.

questBrief.mission: Use pattern memory to choose the response instead of repeating fear.
questBrief.fieldPrompt: Notice which old pattern no longer requires the old defense.
questBrief.livePrompt: What can be recognized without reacting?

fieldBriefing.collapsedPreview: Do not obey the old fear.
fieldBriefing.whatThisQuestIsAbout: This line sees recurring patterns from a wider distance. It can recognize what has happened before without automatically becoming trapped in the old survival response.
fieldBriefing.whyThisMattersNow: Mature recognition creates choice. Immature recognition reactivates the past.
fieldBriefing.primaryRecognitionTrigger: You call it instinct, but it is the old defense running early.
fieldBriefing.livePrompt: Where are you reacting to memory instead of the present?

assetsAndFriction.questTrap: You protect yourself from a pattern that is not fully here.
frictionSignals: treating old pain as current evidence
assetsAndFriction.livePrompt: Watch where memory arrives disguised as present danger.

groundingProtocol: lengthen the back of the neck
groundingEffect.somaticShift: The body recognizes the echo without collapsing into the old response.
groundingEffect.bodyCheckPrompt: Can your spine stay present while memory speaks?

unlockCondition.questKey: Recognize the old pattern → confirm the present evidence → respond only to what is real now
unlockCondition.embodiedSignal: The body feels alert without being hijacked by the past.
unlockCondition.ritualPrompt: Ask what is actually happening now before reacting.
unlockCondition.alignmentReminder: Pattern memory is guidance, not command.
unlockCondition.livePrompt: Respond to the present, not the echo.

perkTrees.subtitle: Develop the capacities behind Foundational Pattern Recognition.

nodes.name: Pattern Memory
nodes.description: This path develops the ability to recognize recurring patterns through foundational pattern recognition.
nodes.effect: Instinctive memory becomes useful when it is verified, proportionate, and acted on cleanly.
nodes.developmentCue: Notice whether recognition is present-time signal or old fear.
nodes.masteryMarker: You can recognize patterns without becoming controlled by them.

nodes.name: First-Signal Recognition
nodes.description: You learn to detect the pattern before it becomes entanglement.
nodes.effect: Familiar dynamics become visible earlier and with less distortion.
nodes.developmentCue: Track the first familiar signal without finalizing the story.
nodes.masteryMarker: You can separate recognition from assumption.

nodes.name: Instinctive Patience
nodes.description: The body learns to stay present while instinctive memory activates.
nodes.effect: Recognition no longer automatically becomes defense.
nodes.developmentCue: Let the body verify the signal before reacting.
nodes.masteryMarker: You remain physically alert without tightening into old fear.

nodes.name: Pattern Verification
nodes.description: The relational field becomes easier to read when the pattern is named cleanly.
nodes.effect: Old dynamics can be interrupted before they silently run the room.
nodes.developmentCue: Observe how the pattern moves between people.
nodes.masteryMarker: You can name the dynamic without taking it over.

nodes.name: Clean Recognition
nodes.description: The full path matures into pattern recognition that creates choice instead of reaction.
nodes.effect: The past becomes information rather than command.
nodes.developmentCue: Respond to the current pattern, not the old echo.
nodes.masteryMarker: You can meet familiar dynamics without repeating them.
